flashcards.q: Term: A known fixed quantity used for comparison.
Term: Distance between the tip of the thumb and the little finger.
Term: The universally accepted system of measurement units.
Unit: The SI unit of length.
Conversion: How many metres are in 1 kilometre (km)?
Conversion: How many centimetres (cm) are in 1 metre?
Conversion: How many millimetres (mm) are in 1 centimetre?
Conversion: How many centimetres are in 1 inch?
History: Name three ancient Indian units of length.
Error: What is the reading error caused by viewing a scale from a side angle?
Correct Method: Where should your eye be while reading a scale marking?
Correct Method: How to measure an object with a broken zero mark on a scale?
flashcards.a: Start from another full mark and subtract its value from the final reading.

flashcards.q: Tool: What device is used to measure the girth of a tree or chest size?
Visually Challenged: How do visually impaired students read scales?
flashcards.a: Using scales with raised markings that can be felt by touch.

flashcards.q: Advanced Tool: Device used for professional road race measurement.
Curved Boundaries: How can you measure the length of a curved line exactly?
flashcards.a: Using a thread to trace the curve, then measuring the thread on a scale.

flashcards.q: Position: A fixed object or point used to describe location.
Definition: An object is in this state if its position changes with time.
Definition: An object is in this state if its position stays fixed with time.
Term: Point or object relative to which motion is decided.
Motion: Movement along a straight line.
Motion: Movement along a circular path.
Motion: To-and-fro movement about a fixed position.
Motion: Movement that repeats itself at regular time intervals.
Example: What type of motion does a falling orange exhibit?
Example: What kind of motion does a swing in a park show?
Logic: Are circular and oscillatory motions periodic in nature?
Rule: Should unit symbols like km or m be written in capital letters?
flashcards.a: No, they should be in lowercase (except when starting a sentence).

flashcards.q: Rule: Should a full stop be written after a unit symbol (e.g., cm.)?
flashcards.a: No (unless it is the end of a sentence).

flashcards.q: Numerical: If a wheel circumfrence is 1m, how far is 10 turns?
